text:
Once upon a time in the tranquil village of Eldergrove, there lived a fox named Fletchy. Fletchy loved nothing more than lounging on the cool riverbank, nibbling on juicy wildberries. One serene sun noonday, a strange thing happened. A small white bird fluttered down from the towering cherry trees. It chirped softly and looked at Fletchy with big, curious eyes. Then it began to sing a dainty little melody.

Fletchy watched for a moment. The bird rested on a bush, chirped melodiously again, and slowly flew up high. Fletchy tilted his head. That was unlike any bird he'd ever heard.

Later, Fletchy would hear the song often. The first day he heard it, he thought it was a strange wind. But each visitation made him chuckle. By nighttime, Fletchy would remember those songs all year long.

One autumn eve, snow began to crunch softly underfoot.
